Procedural Music Generation documentation¶

Go back to examples page

This is the documentation for the Procedural Music Generation project. It includes various modules and examples to help you get started with generating music procedurally.

Music module¶

class core.music.Key(tonic: str, mode: str = 'major')[source]¶

Bases: object

A musical key, defined by its tonic and mode.

tonic¶

Root note of the key (‘c’, ‘eb’, ‘fs’, etc.)

mode¶

Scale type (‘major’, ‘minor’, ‘dorian’, etc.)

degree_to_pitch(degree: str) → str[source]¶
get_scale_pitches()[source]¶

Returns: A list of strings representing the pitch names of the scale degrees in the key.

class core.music.Melody(key: Key, notes, tags=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

A musical melody, consisting of a sequence of notes and an associated key.

key¶

Key object representing the key of the melody.

notes¶

List of Note objects representing the melody.

tags¶

optional dictionary for additional metadata (e.g., verse, original/variation, bass/tenor, etc.)

add_rhythm(rhythm)[source]¶

Adds rhythm to the melody by assigning durations to each note based on a provided rhythm pattern.

Parameters:

rhythm (list) – A list of durations corresponding to each note in the melody.

Raises:

ValueError – If the length of the rhythm list does not match the number of notes in the melody.

to_pitches()[source]¶

Converts abstract notes (with scale degrees) to concrete pitches based on the melody’s key.

Raises:

ValueError – If the melody has no key assigned or if any note is abstract without a degree.

class core.music.Note(degree=None, pitch=None, duration=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

A musical note, represented by scale degree, concrete pitch and duration.

degree¶

Scale degree (1, 2, b3, s4, etc.) (Abstract)

pitch¶

Concrete pitch (‘c’, ‘eb’, ‘fs’, etc.) (Concrete)

duration¶

Duration in beats (1 for quarter note, 0.5 for eighth note, etc.)

is_abstract()[source]¶

Utility function to check if the note is abstract (has a degree but no concrete pitch).

Returns: True if the note has a degree but no concrete pitch, False otherwise.

is_concrete()[source]¶

Utility function to check if the note is concrete (has a concrete pitch).

Returns: True if the note has a concrete pitch, False otherwise.

core.music.scale_degrees_to_pitch(degrees, root)[source]¶

Converting scale degrees to pitch names based on the root note.

Parameters:

  • degrees (list) – List of string representing the scale degrees.

  • root (str) – String representing the root of the key for the melody. (‘c’, ‘eb’, ‘fs’, etc.)

Returns: A list of strings representing pitch names.

core.music.spell_note(letter, accidental)[source]¶

Helper function to spell a note based on its letter and accidental.

Parameters:

  • letter (str) – A string representing note to be spelled.

  • accidental (int) – Integer representing accidental. (-2, -1, 0, 1, 2) Negative for flats and positive for sharps.

Returns: A string with the spelled note.

Lilypond module¶

out.lilypond.beat_to_duration(beat)[source]¶

Convert a duration in beats to the corresponding LilyPond duration notation.

Parameters:

beat (float) – Duration in beats (e.g., 1 for quarter note, 0.5 for eighth note, etc.)

Returns: Corresponding LilyPond duration notation as a string.

out.lilypond.generate_files(filename='output.ly', path='')[source]¶

Generate MIDI and PDF files from the .ly file using LilyPond.

Parameters:

  • filename (str) – Name of the .ly file to be processed.

  • path (str) – Optional path where the generated files will be saved.

out.lilypond.melody_to_lilypond(melody: Melody, voice_name, clef)[source]¶

Convert a Melody object to LilyPond format.

Parameters:

  • voice_name (str) – Name of the voice to be used in LilyPond (e.g., “voiceOne”).

  • clef (str) – Clef to be used in LilyPond (e.g., “treble”, “bass”, “”treble_8””).

  • melody – Melody object to be converted to LilyPond format.

Returns: A tuple containing the LilyPond code for the melody and the corresponding staff reference for the score.

Raises:

ValueError – If the melody has no key assigned, if any note has no pitch assigned, or if any note has no duration assigned.

out.lilypond.play(filename='output.ly')[source]¶

Play generated MIDI file using Timidity. Assumes that the MIDI file has been generated from the .ly file and is in the same directory.

Parameters:

filename (str) – The name of the .ly file from which the MIDI file was generated. The function will look for a corresponding .midi file with the same base name.

out.lilypond.voices_to_lilypond(voices, key, time_sig)[source]¶

Write all voices into a single LilyPond score.

Parameters:

  • voices (list) – List of tuples containing LilyPond code for each voice and their staff references.

  • key (str) – The key to notate the score in.

  • time_sig (str) – The time signature to notate the score in.

Returns:

out.lilypond.write_to_file(lilypond_code, filename='output.ly')[source]¶

Write the generated LilyPond code to a .ly file.

Parameters:

  • lilypond_code (str) – The LilyPond code to be written to the file.

  • filename (str) – The name of the .ly file to write to. Defaults to “output.ly”.

Generator module¶

generators.generator.generate_notes(num_notes=4, key: ~core.music.Key = Key(c major)) → Melody[source]¶

Generate a melody by randomly moving in 2nds and 3rds within the scale.

Parameters:

  • num_notes (int) – Number of notes to generate in the melody.

  • key – Key object representing the key of the melody.

Returns:

generators.generator.generate_random_notes(length=8, key: ~core.music.Key = Key(c major)) → Melody[source]¶

Generate a random chromatic melody.

Parameters:

  • length (int) – Number of notes in the melody.

  • key – Key object representing the key to display the melody in.

Returns: A Melody object containing the generated notes and the associated key.

generators.generator.generate_rhythm(bars=1, time_sig='4/4', values=['q'])[source]¶

Generate a rhythm pattern based on the time signature and allowed rhythm values.

Parameters:

  • bars (int) – Number of bars to generate.

  • time_sig (str) – Time signature in the format “quantity/value” (e.g., “4/4”, “3/4”, “6/8”).

  • values (list) – List of rhythm symbols (e.g., [“q”, “e”, “h”]) that can be used in the rhythm pattern.

Returns: A list of rhythm values corresponding to the generated rhythm pattern.

generators.generator.rhythm_to_values(rhythm)[source]¶

Convert rhythm symbols to beat values.

Parameters:

rhythm (list) – List of rhythm symbols (e.g., [“q”, “e”, “h”]).

Returns:

Procedural Music Generation
